name: Old Fashioned Coconut Custard Pie
recipeIngredient: Sweet Dough -- for a 1 crust pie,
Tiny pinch of freshly grated nutmeg
recipeInstructions:
Prepare and chill the dough.
Set a rack at the middle level of the oven and preheat to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
Use a large nail or an ice pick to puncture one of the three eyes on the coconut (only one can be punctured, so you may need to try each until you find it). Drain the coconut milk over a bowl. Strain it to remove any flecks of shell and reserve it.
Place the coconut on the oven rack and bake it until it cracks, about 10 to 15 minutes.
Remove the coconut from the oven and let it cool slightly. Remove the shell (it will come away easily), then cut the coconut meat into 8 or 10 pieces. With a vegetable peeler, remove the brown skin. Coarsely grate 1 cup of coconut, either by hand or in the food processor, and reserve it for the filling. Process the remaining coconut until it is very fine.
In a large saucepan over low heat, scald the milk until there are bubbles around the edges. Add the finely processed coconut. Remove from the heat and allow the mixture to cool completely. Pour it through a fine strainer into a bowl; press coconut to extract as much moisture as possible.
In 3 or 4 batches, place the coconut in the corner of a clean dish towel and wring out over the bowl to extract all remaining liquid. Measure the coconut-flavored milk and add more milk if necessary, to make 2 cups. This can be done earlier in the day or even the day before and set aside tightly covered in the refrigerator.
Pour into a bowl and whisk in sugar, eggs, vanilla, and nutmeg and the reserved coconut milk. Stir in the reserved coarsely grated coconut.
Set a rack in the lowest level of the oven and preheat to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Roll out the dough for the bottom crust and arrange it in the pan.
Pour filling into pie crust. Bake the pie for about 45 minutes, until the crust is baked through and the filling is set, but not over-cooked. After the pie has baked for 30 minutes, check it frequently so it does not overcook. Once the pie is wobbly when the pan is gently shaken, remove it from the oven; it will continue to cook for a few minutes longer after being removed from the oven. If the pie is overbaked, it will be heavy and watery. Cool the pie on a rack.
OLD FASHIONED CUSTARD PIE:
Omit coconut. Add 1/2 cup of heavy or light cream to the milk. Mix sugar with 1 teaspoon of cornstarch before adding to the milk. Bake as above.
